display.purpose: Article titles and major page titles.

headline.purpose: Post titles and strong section headings.

title.purpose: Site subtitle and local section headings.

label.purpose: Navigation, tags, compact metadata, and code-adjacent labels.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Small hover color transitions on links and controls.

components.name: Text Link
components.description: Default inline link with ink text, mauve underline, and a tiny hover tint.
components.css: .ds-link { color: #252525; text-decoration-color: #67624a; text-decoration-thickness: 0.15ex; text-underline-offset: 0.3ex; transition: background-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-link:hover { background-color: rgba(103, 98, 74, 0.1); border-radius: 2px; } .ds-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #67624a; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Navigation
components.description: Sticky lowercase text navigation with translucent paper backing.
components.css: .ds-nav { position: sticky; top: 0; text-align: left; background: rgba(250, 250, 250, 0.7); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(1px); backdrop-filter: blur(1px); font-family: 'iA Writer Mono', monospace; } .ds-nav a { color: #252525; margin: 25px 0; padding-right: 12px; text-decoration-color: #67624a; text-decoration-thickness: 0.15ex; text-underline-offset: 0.3ex; } .ds-nav a:hover { background-color: transparent; } .ds-nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #67624a; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Tag
components.description: Inline hash tag used for browsing posts by subject.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-block; margin-right: 20px; color: #252525; font-family: 'iA Writer Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.8rem; text-decoration-color: #67624a; text-underline-offset: 0.3ex; } .ds-tag:hover { cursor: pointer; font-weight: 700; } .ds-tag:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #67624a; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Post List Item
components.description: A document-like post entry that uses spacing and type instead of a card container.
components.html:
AI Software Engineers Don't Exist

June 1, 2026

A compact description of the post.
components.css: .ds-post { margin: 0 0 30px 0; color: #252525; font-family: 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif; } .ds-post h2 { margin: 0 0 0.25em; color: #6f6f6a; font-family: 'iA Writer Mono', monospace; font-size: 1.3125rem; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.15; } .ds-post a { color: #252525; text-decoration-color: #67624a; text-decoration-thickness: 0.15ex; text-underline-offset: 0.3ex; } .ds-post time { font-style: italic; color: #6f6f6a; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-post p { line-height: 1.6; margin: 0.5em 0 0; }

narrative.overview:
This design system should feel like a personal notebook kept by a programmer who cares about craft: text-led, slightly old-world, technically literate, and more interested in durable thought than presentation fashion. The current site is intentionally compact, centered, and readable. It uses type, rhythm, and a small number of color decisions rather than layout spectacle.

The system rejects trend-chasing by staying close to the writing. It should not become a SaaS landing page, an influencer portfolio, a generic developer template, or a fashionable editorial costume. Polish is welcome when it sharpens the reading experience; decoration is suspect unless it helps curiosity.
keyCharacteristics: Near-white proof paper and dense ink, intentionally light-mode only.
Source Serif 4 carries long-form prose; iA Writer Mono marks headings, navigation, code, and labels.
Flat surfaces, no cards by default, no ambient shadows.
Compact navigation and tag affordances that invite browsing without turning the site into an app shell.
Small, handmade details are allowed; trendy visual systems are not.

rules.name: The Accent Scarcity Rule
rules.body: Printer's Olive should appear in small doses: link underlines, quotes, and state feedback. If it starts filling large panels, the system has become too designed.

rules.name: The Reading Contrast Rule
rules.body: Body text must stay high contrast against the active paper color. Never use Printer's Gray for paragraph text.

rules.name: The Prose First Rule
rules.body: Body copy is the authority. Do not let mono headings become oversized branding artifacts.

rules.name: The No Costume Mono Rule
rules.body: Monospace is for structure and technical texture only. Do not use it as lazy shorthand for "developer".

rules.name: The Flat Reading Rule
rules.body: Do not add card shadows to make ordinary content feel important. If a surface needs emphasis, use hierarchy, spacing, or a stronger heading.

dos: Do keep the body column around 80ch and let the writing own the page.
Do use Source Serif 4 for prose and iA Writer Mono for headings, navigation, code, and compact labels.
Do keep Printer's Olive scarce and purposeful.
Do keep the site light-mode only unless dark mode is deliberately reintroduced.
Do use browsing paths such as posts, talks, tags, and RSS to encourage curiosity.
donts: Don't make the site feel trendy, growth-hacked, SaaS-like, influencer-coded, or visually optimized for whatever design pattern is currently fashionable.
Don't turn post lists into repeated marketing cards.
Don't add purple gradients, glassmorphism, hero metrics, or generic developer-template ornament.
Don't use Printer's Gray for paragraph text.
Don't replace the handmade personal-site feeling with a product dashboard shell.
